summary: Large tooling-driven cleanup addressing issues reported by `melee-issues`: renamed address-derived item source/asm files, updated `obj_files.mk`, added many item headers/prototypes with GALE01-style include guards, removed broad local extern lists by including proper headers, and fixed warning/prototype/type issues across item, fighter, Dolphin DVD, and sysdolphin baselib code. No PR body or review discussion was present in the slice, so intent is inferred from the title and diff only.
what_changed: Renamed several item asm/source paths from full-address or `itunk` names to shorter address/module names, e.g. `asm/melee/it/it_803F1418.s` to `asm/melee/it/it_3F14.s`, `it_803F23CC` to `it_3F23`, `it_803F3100` to `it_3F31`, and `itunk1/2/4` to `it_2ADA`, `it_2F28`, and `it_2E5A`.
Updated `obj_files.mk` to match the renamed item source/asm object paths so the build still links the same code under the new names.
Added `-Wno-tautological-bitwise-compare` to `compile_flags.txt`, matching the warning-cleanup nature of the PR.

Reworked `src/melee/it/it_3F14.c` heavily: replaced hundreds of local `extern` declarations with includes for item headers, introduced `static int const count = 43`, and used that count for item GX-link and logic tables.
Added many item headers under `src/melee/it/items/`, using GALE01-address include guards and function/table declarations so shared item tables can include real declarations instead of ad-hoc externs.
Expanded `it_266F.h`, created `it_2725.h`, and adjusted `it_27CF.h` declarations, including corrected signatures such as `it_8026EECC(HSD_GObj*, int)` and `it_80273030` returning `bool`.

Standardized render callback naming in GObj-related structs and code from `render_cb` to `rendered`, and introduced `GObj_RenderFunc` in `baselib/forward.h`. This propagated through `HSD_GObj`, fighter/item forward structs, `render_gobj`, and `GObj_SetupGXLink*`.
Fixed old-style function declarations by adding explicit `void` parameters, e.g. `__DVDFSInit(void)`, `HSD_CObjEndCurrent(void)`, and `HSD_PerfCountMtxLoad(void)`.
Changed several callback/code parameters from `s32` or `long` to `int`, including `cmSnap_80031640`, `ftDrawCommmon_80080E18`, and `grDisplay_801C5DB0`, apparently to align callback signatures.
Fixed signed/unsigned comparison warnings by casting before comparing against unsigned lengths in `dvdfs.c` and `fobj.c`.

Added `MUST_MATCH`-guarded unused stack padding and guarded duplicate assignments in places like `ftPr_Init_UnkMtxFunc0`, `HSD_FObjInterpretAnim`, and camera/object code, preserving matching behavior while keeping non-matching builds cleaner.
Adjusted baselib camera/object code toward clearer C types and flags, e.g. using `float`, `(1 << 30) | (1 << 31)` flag expressions, and wrapping some inline asm declarations in `MWERKS_GEKKO` guards.
Moved/reordered includes in some fighter/item users of `itpeachparasol.h` and related item headers, likely to satisfy include-order or self-contained-header checks.
smart_moves: Using real item headers instead of massive local extern blocks in item table files makes future table work easier to audit and reduces signature drift.
The GALE01-address include guard convention gives anonymous/address-named item headers stable, collision-resistant guards even before better semantic names are known.
Renaming unknown item asm/source files from `itunk*` or full function-address filenames to compact module-offset names preserves uncertainty while making paths more systematic and searchable.
Introducing `GObj_RenderFunc` centralizes the render callback signature rather than repeating raw function-pointer types across `gobj`, `gobjgxlink`, fighter, and item forward declarations.
Separating `MUST_MATCH`-only stack padding and duplicate-assignment tricks from normal C paths keeps matching hacks explicit without burdening non-matching builds.
Casting signed offsets to unsigned only at the comparison sites fixes compiler diagnostics while preserving the apparent intended bounds checks against unsigned file lengths.
Replacing magic flag masks like `0xC0000000` with bit expressions such as `(1 << 30) | (1 << 31)` improves readability without changing the stored bit pattern.
decomp_lessons: When tooling flags missing prototypes, prefer adding self-contained headers and includes over accumulating file-local extern declarations; it prevents mismatched signatures across translation units.
For unknown item modules, address-based names such as `it_3F14`, `it_2ADA`, and GALE01 include guards are a useful intermediate convention until semantic names are proven.
Callback fields in shared structs should use one typedef and one signature; otherwise `s32`, `long`, and `int` drift can produce warnings or ABI ambiguity.
Old-style empty parameter lists should be converted to `(void)` for functions with no parameters, especially in SDK/baselib code that is included broadly.
If matching requires unused stack or odd assignments, isolate them behind `MUST_MATCH` and annotate with `@todo Unused stack` so later decompilers know the padding is intentional.
For comparisons between signed offsets and unsigned lengths, explicit casts document the intended domain and silence modern compiler checks without changing control flow.
Build metadata like `obj_files.mk` must be updated in lockstep with file renames; pure renames still affect object ordering and symbol placement in this project.
naming_conventions: Unknown/addressed item source files use shortened address-module names such as `it_3F14.c`, `it_3F23.c`, and `it_3F31.c` rather than full first-symbol names like `it_803F1418.c`.
Unknown item asm files in `items/` use names like `it_2ADA.s`, `it_2E5A.s`, `it_2E6A.s`, `it_2F28.s`, and `it_2F2B.s` instead of `itunkN.s`.
Include guards for address-based files follow `GALE01_<address>` style, e.g. `GALE01_26C47C`, `GALE01_272560`, `GALE01_27CF30`, and many item guards such as `GALE01_2ADA1C`.
GObj render callback member naming was changed from `render_cb` to `rendered`, with callback parameter names like `code` and `gobj`.
New item table cleanup used a shared `static int const count = 43` for common-item table lengths instead of repeating the literal in multiple declarations.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Renamed asm files with 100% similarity, indicating path normalization without assembly content changes.
Used `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` blocks for unused stack buffers, e.g. `u8 _[4] = { 0 };` or `u8 _[8] = { 0 };`, instead of leaving unused locals active in all builds.
Used `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` duplicate assignments such as `state = state = 3` only in matching builds, with ordinary assignments in the non-matching branch.
Wrapped several inline asm functions in `#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO`, keeping non-Metrowerks builds from seeing raw MWERKS asm.
Changed asm prototype spellings to match C-side signatures, such as `asm void ftDrawCommmon_80080E18(HSD_GObj*, int)` and `asm void HSD_CObjSetUpVector(void)`.
Preserved item table ordering while changing formatting/comments and replacing extern declarations with header-driven declarations.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that continue converting item-local externs into `src/melee/it/items/*.h` declarations.
Check whether `rendered` remained the accepted field name for GObj render callbacks or was renamed again in later baselib cleanup.
Search subsequent PRs for more `GALE01_` include-guard conversions to confirm this became the convention for address-based headers.
Investigate whether the `-Wno-tautological-bitwise-compare` flag was temporary or remained necessary after later warning fixes.
Find later decomp work on `it_3F14.c`, `it_3F23.c`, and `it_3F31.c` to see how these table/header cleanups enabled matching or semantic renames.
